15/Apr/26 2:05 PM Unified Platform Sprint 14 Release Done Provider Maintenance Accepted   Critical SCPDataMigration, SCPHypercareBug, SCPPRODTicket, TADesignComplete David Wilkening   PR1050558-4954, PR1050558-5604, PR1050558-6348, PR1050558-6349, PR1050558-6354, PR1050558-5648, PR1050558-6235 Ashlee Johnson-Savoy Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-5792
No permission
 Robert Christensen PR1050558-6274, PR1050558-6305, PR1050558-6306 *As an Admin, I can update an HPF Facility’s Facility Title (CodeSet) lookup value to reflect that a clinician’s role at a particular HPF facility has changed but not ended.*

Currently the batch close process that deactivates HPFs on their “Effective To” dates assumes the total end of the relationship so the Termination Date is flagged which results in closing all other HPFs. This is a problem when the HPF is only expiring because a NEW HPF was created to represent a change in Facility Title. (Consider a MD that takes a role as a Director of Medicine at a Facility for which they currently work, but only at that one Facility. On all others they remain a MD)



* *Solution*
** *Add HPF.Status value “Superseded”*
** *Update Flow(s)*
*** *Healthcare Practitioner Facility After Insert Flow, HPF Updates Triggered Flow both uses sub flow Update Active HPF to Inactive*
**** Update the old HPF Status Value to “Superseded” in *Update Active HPF to Inactive*
*** *Update Previous HPF on contract Activation Scheduled Flow, Assigning variables to Update record*
**** HPF Status Value to “Superseded” and *remove* update to fields TerminationDate

** *Update Flow(s)*
*** *Update Previous HPF Records*
**** Update the old HPF Status Value to “Superseded” in *Update Active HPF to Inactive*
**** HPF Status Value to “Superseded” and *remove* update to fields ,TerminationDate
** *Update Flow(s)*
*** *Update Previous HPF on contract Activation Scheduled Flow*
**** Update the old HPF Status Value to “Superseded” in *Update Active HPF to Inactive*
**** HPF Status Value to “Superseded” and *remove* update to fields TerminationDate



* *Add HPF.Status value “Superseded”*
* *Update Flow(s)*
** *Update Previous HPFRecords*
*** Update the old HPF Status Value to “Superseded”

Update Formula Field: Provider System Status

to include the logic

when “HPF.Status = ‘Superseded’ and Effective To > (TODAY())” = ‘Active’ 		0 Robert Christensen 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Update old HPF Status to ‘Superseded’ vs 'Inactive and DO NOT populate ‘Termination Date’ for Facility Title Change - Updates story 4954, updates in {color:#403294}purple{color}
Given I am a Recruiter in the Corporate Support apps
When a clinician contract has a HPF included where the clinician has an active HPF (Status ≠ ‘Inactive’) for that same Service Account (ex. Facility Title change)
Then when the Contract is Executed the following updates happen automatically to the Old HPF*:

* Contracting Status = ‘Termination In Progress’
* Anticipated Termination Date = <Newly Executed Clinician Contract’s ‘Contract Start Date’ - 1>

And then on the newly executed Clinician Contract ‘Contract Start Date’ (Contract Start Date = Today or in the past), then the system shall update the following fields on the Old HPF*:

* Status = {color:#403294}-‘Inactive’- {color}{color:#403294}‘Superseded’{color}
* Contracting Status = ‘Terminated’
* {color:#403294}-Termination Date = <new contract start date - 1>- {color}{color:#403294}[REMOVE this update, Termination Date should be blank]{color}

*Old HPF record(s) are defined as: the HPF in Status ≠ Inactive for the same Service Account


AC2: Update HPF formula field = ‘Provider System Status' to support new status = ‘Superseded’
Given I am a System Administrator within the the Corporate Support Apps
When the HPF ‘Status’ = ‘Superseded’
And ‘Effective To’ is greater than Today’s date
Then set ‘Provider System Status’ = ‘Active’ 		15/Apr/26 2:05 PM   7 HPF Facility Title change with termination date Qgenda Data.png 19/Feb/26 3:51 PM SCP HC Sprint 14 (Phase 2), SCP HC Sprint 12.4 (R2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 15 (Phase 2) 19/Feb/26 5:28 PM 1|i0knxs:zzv 15/Apr/26 2:05 PM *Tester:* Heather Clavier

 1|i0m6jd: 14/Apr/26 2:49 PM *Tester:* Kristi Bouillion

*Role:* Medical Staff Liaison – CPA

*Description:* 

"he Review CPA Relationship section is not displaying any of the physicians at the location, for the the MSL-CPA team to modify relationships- below are two examples. This was working in UAT and is not working in production.

This is business critical as they are unable to perform some of their duties and we are at risk of not knowing if we have the proper CPA relationships in place. There is also a question from the team that I do not have the answer for: When MD/DOs are created, does the system only create one case for each location? If so that is good to know.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Correct the bug and when you navigate to the Review CPA Relationship section all physicians at the account will be displayed.


*Expected vs. Actual:*

The Review CPA Relationship section is not displaying any of the physicians at the location, for the the MSL-CPA team to modify relationships. Correct the bug and when you navigate to the Review CPA Relationship section all physicians at the account will be displayed.

*Record Link:* 

N/A

*Business Impact:*

This is business critical as they are unable to perform some of their duties and we are at risk of not knowing if we have the proper CPA relationships in place. They also cannot populate their required forms for the clinicians to sign.



----

Bug split into 2 parts:
1. [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5791|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5791|smart-link] *Immediate/Prod only fixs:* fix current records and ensure newly Executed contract CPA get generated.
2. [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6062|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6062|smart-link] Fix for all *other items* identified in our investigation.

NOTE: actions to be taken on each bug are currently listed as Sub tasks

 Robert Christensen 0 Robert Christensen Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 26/Feb/26 9:10 AM   5 19/Feb/26 11:15 AM SCP HC Sprint 12.4 (R2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3) 19/Feb/26 12:58 PM Working as expected
Other
1|i0m5j5: 02/Mar/26 5:48 PM *Tester:* Lisa Kleyla

*Role:* Payroll

*Description:* 

Provider: Gregory Dorilus GID 811310
Provider has a new contract record effective 6/1/2026. It was fully executed on 2/18/2026. Hire date field on contract record is populated as 6/1/2026.

Hire date should be populated in one of two ways, when a provider's first clinical shift is populated on the HPF or if the start admin flag ahead of clinical is check, the hire date will populate as his administrative start date.

In this scenario, first clinical shift is blank and the start admin work ahead of clinical check box is not checked. Confirmed he is also not scheduled in QGenda. With the hire date populated, it triggered an integration to Workday prematurely. Manual input of this field should not be allowed so unsure how it was populated

*Steps to Reproduce:*

See above


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Hire date should remain blank until the first clinical shift is populated (hire date = first clinical shift) or the start admin work ahead of clinical work is checked (hire date = admin start date)

*Record Link:* 

Contract:
[https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001FrQknIAF/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FReport%2F00OPs000006A03JMAS%2Fview%3FqueryScope%3DuserFolders|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001FrQknIAF/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FReport%2F00OPs000006A03JMAS%2Fview%3FqueryScope%3DuserFolders]

Deployment and Configuration: Deployment Errors
1|i0m49t: 26/Feb/26 9:47 AM *Tester:* Murphy Alkhatib

*Role:* Recruiter

*Description:* 

Recruiter received an email from Kristi Bouillion regarding some data clean up. Clinician Antonya Williams HPF was inactivated and she was fully termed in QGenda. Kristi requested we create a new HPF and get an agreement out to clinician ASAP to avoid interruption in payroll. Recruiter attempted to create a new HPF with effective date 2/1/26 and it didn't work. Attempted again with 2/2/2026 effective date thinking the same effective date on inactive HPF was causing an issue. It still didn't work.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Outlined above


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Since the HPF is in inactive status, should be able to create a new HPF

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001Ps00001KlXd7IAF/view|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001Ps00001KlXd7IAF/view|smart-link]

*OS:* ServiceAccFacilityRateCreation

# Shift Type “*Call*“ will allow NPPA only Unit per Measure: “per Shift“ *AND* “per Hour“
## Error message 1 verbatim for validation

*Notes:* Shift Type “Call“ is available and active in Dev1 and higher orgs

CC. [~accountid:712020:c808bf84-f5db-43dc-9193-481dde8463e1] 		0 Robert Christensen 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Updates story [+PR1050558-2018+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2018], updates in {color:#0747a6}blue{color} to update Shift Type = ‘Call’

Given I am a SFO in the corporate support apps
When I configure rates for a Practice Operations contract in Status = Draft
And I am specifying shift rates for Employee NP/PA (Type of Rate = ‘Shift', Shift Type = ‘NP/PA’, & Employment Type = ‘Employed’)
Then for ALL Shift Types *EXCEPT* for the following, I must select Unit of Measure = ‘per Hour’, if not display error message = ‘Unit of measure has to be per Hour NP/PA Employee Shift rate'

* Shift Type (AKA 'Shift Name') = ‘*Shift Bonus*’, then user must select Unit of Measure = “per Shift” (see story 2430 - ”Shift Bonus requires 'Per Shift' as the Unit of Measure. Please update to proceed. Alternatively, select individual shifts to choose a different Unit of Measure.”)
* {color:#0747a6}Shift Type (AKA 'Shift Name') = ‘{color}{color:#0747a6}*Call*{color}{color:#0747a6}', then user can select Unit of Measure = ‘per Shift’ or ‘per Hour’. {color}
** {color:#0747a6}Error message 1 (unique): ‘Unit of measure for ‘Call’ has to be ‘per Hour’ or ‘per Shift’ for NP/PA Employee Shift rate'{color}
** {color:#0747a6}-Error message 2 (generic):-{color} {color:#0747a6}-‘Unit of measure has to be ‘per Hour’ for NP/PA Employee Shift rate; except for ‘Shift Bonus' or ‘Call’ which can be ‘per Shift’.’-{color} 		04/Mar/26 4:42 PM   7 Shift NPPA unit of measure.png 18/Feb/26 12:57 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 12.6 (R4) 18/Feb/26 1:09 PM 1|i0m3oh: 04/Mar/26 4:42 PM *Tester:* Lisa Kleyla

*Role:* Payroll

*Description:* 

Under the shift rate, we are not able to select a unit of measure of "per shift" for a NPPA call rate.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

From the PO contract link below, click specify rates for an employed NPPA and select Call 1 (NPPA) and attempt to select the unit of measure of "per shift".


*Expected vs. Actual:*

We need to select per shift for certain rates, such as call shifts.

*Record Link*

[https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/omnistudio/omniscript?omniscript__type=ServiceAcc&omniscript__subType=FacilityRateCreation&omniscript__language=English&omniscript__theme=lightning&omniscript__recordId=800Ps00001GAVTqIAP&omniscript__tabLabel=00471756 | Contract&omniscript__recordTypeDeveloperName=Client|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/omnistudio/omniscript?omniscript__type=ServiceAcc&omniscript__subType=FacilityRateCreation&omniscript__language=English&omniscript__theme=lightning&omniscript__recordId=800Ps00001GAVTqIAP&omniscript__tabLabel=00471756%20%7C%20Contract&omniscript__recordTypeDeveloperName=Client]

1|i0m1j5: 02/Mar/26 5:49 PM *Tester:* Bryan Wade

*Role:* N/A

*Description:* 

The "old" BI process that used to update the facility information (Refresh OnBase Facility List on LAFING1.SalesforceData that populates the FacilityList table on LAFIDB1.OnBase) was taken out back and shot for the ReGenesis upgrade. Post-upgrade, it was discovered that the data refresh had not been happening. We reached out to BI to have it run manually to update the FacilityList table, and now things is all broke, bruh. At the moment, what we know is listed below in the business impact statement. Tyler is currently working with Gerardo on remediation and I believe he has also reached out to someone on the upgrade team about this issue. This case is our official notification that we don't seem to be in Kansas anymore, Toto.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

This is happening in production now


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Some documents are not processing correctly, business processes are interrupted to a degree, EZDI is reporting getting strange unknown facility process calls, and that's all we know right now.

*Record Link:* 

PROD Bug: UPHypercare229 - OnBase Facility List Issues

 Robert Christensen PR1050558-6184 h3. *AC1: Start New Contract Request*

* User enters *Contract Start Date* and *Description* (required). (first screen - existing functionality)
* When the message _"No previous contract found to perform a change to clone from"_ appears, add a *"Start New Contract Request"* option *when the query don't return anything to clone from*.
* *Finish/Save:* Create a new PO Contract (Draft, Practice Operations, Owner = Account Owner, Previous Contract = blank) and navigate to it.
* *Cancel:* Close the guided experience and return to the Service Account.
* *Implementation:* Add a conditional branch in {{Clinician_ChangeRequest_English_2}}, create an Integration Procedure to create the contract, and wire Finish/Save and Cancel.



h3. *C2: Clone from Superseded Contract*

* *Selection rule:* Use PO contracts with Status = Executed or Superseded, ordered by *Start Date dec* (newest first).
* Choose the contract where the user’s *Contract Start Date* falls within its period: {{StartDate <= user date}} AND ({{EndDate >= user date}} OR {{EndDate}} is blank).
* *Implementation:* Update {{DRGetPOContract}} to use {{ORDER BY StartDate Dec}} and adjust filters for EndDate (including blank).
* Step 2 already shows Contract Number, Start Date, Status, and Coverage Change; confirm it uses the new selection.



h3. *AC3: Update End Date and Status on Execution (use a FLOW) and*

* When a PO contract becomes *Executed* and its *Start Date* is today or in the past use a Flow: (ALREDY BUILT)
*# Get all PO contracts for the same Account with Status Executed or Superseded.
*# Sort by Start Date *descending* (newest first).
*# Set each contract’s *End Date* = (next contract’s Start Date − 1 day).
*# Set *Status = Superseded* for any contract whose End Date is in the past.
*# {color:#ff991f}*The End Date should be adjusted for all contract in the list and only update when there is needed a change*{color}

* When a PO contract becomes *Executed* and its *Start Date* is future use a batch job: (ALREADY BUILT)
*# Get all PO contracts for the same Account with Status Executed or Superseded.
*# Sort by Start Date *descending* (newest first).
*# Set each contract’s *End Date* = (next contract’s Start Date − 1 day).
*# Set *Status = Superseded* for any contract whose End Date is in the past.
*# {color:#ff991f}*The End Date should be adjusted for all contract in the list and only update when there is needed a change*{color}
*# However the criteria field should be adjusted as below to pick the record in bath
*## PO_Contract_Scheduled_Flow_Condition:
*### *Prior formula* - IF(RecordType.DeveloperName == ‘Client’ && ISPICKVAL(Status, ‘Executed’) && StartDate == TODAY(), true, false)
*##*Updated formula* - IF(RecordType.DeveloperName == ‘Client’ &&  ISPICKVAL(Status, ‘Executed’) &&  ISBLANK(EndDate) , true, false)



Based on Contract Start Date entered in Previous Screen [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1739|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1739|smart-link]

Contract Start Date = user entered date from screen [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1739|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1739|smart-link]



For All Use Case First Step:

# Check if there is forward contract in the System Query: Contract.RecordType = Practice Operations AND Contract.Status in Draft, Submitted, In Progress AND AccountId = Account from where the button was clicked
## Display All the Contract using the query output above
## Error Message: There is a draft Practice Operation contract under this service account. The system cannot request a contract change at this time. Complete the pending draft Practice Operation contract or cancel the change to proceed further.
# Check if there is a Contract in System Query: Contract.RecordType = Practice Operations AND Contract.Status in Executed, Superseded AND AccountId = [Account from where the button was clicked] AND Contract.StartDate = StartDate for new Contract [Data entry by User]
## Display All the Contract using the query output above
## Error Message: There is a contract for the same ‘Contract Start Date’, either select a different date for the change or first cancel the existing contract and try again.
# Query and Have data ready for next steps
## -Query Contract Record System Query: Contract.RecordType = Practice Operations AND Contract.Status in Executed, Superseded AND AccountId = [Account from where the button was clicked] AND Contract.StartDate >= StartDate for new Contract [Data entry by User] → Get the field Shift_Coverage_Change__c this as- *-FutureShiftCoverageChange-* -= true, false when- *-null(when no Contract exist)-* -then- *-FutureContractDoNotExist-* -= true-
## Query Contract Record System Query: Contract.RecordType = Practice Operations AND Contract.Status in Executed, Superseded AND AccountId = [Account from where the button was clicked] AND Contract.StartDate <= StartDate for new Contract [Data entry by User] ORDER DESC LIMIT 1 → Get the field Shift_Coverage_Change__c this as *PreviousShiftCoverageChange* = true, false when *null(when no Contract exist)* then *PreviousContractDoNotExist* = true
### Query Contract Payment Agreement with ContractId = Contract Id from previous Query AND Type = Shift → Get the field _Mixed_Bag_Shift_Rates__c_ and set the flag *PreviousMixedBagRate* = true when _Mixed_Bag_Shift_Rates__c_ > 0
# When *Are you requesting only Coverage Change?* = Yes [User Entered Input]
## *PreviousMixedBagRate* = true
### Coverage Change cannot be requested contract as there are mixed rate for same shift type go back and select no... See AC for message 		0 Robert Christensen 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Ability to “Start New Contract Request” when no ‘Superseded’ or ‘Executed’ contracts exist.
Given I am a SFO within the Corporate Support Apps
When I am on a Service Account with no Practice Operations (PO) Contracts in Status = ‘Executed’ or ‘Superseded’
And I select guided experience = ‘Request Contract Change’
And on the first step/screen I capture the following required fields: [existing functionality]

* *Are you requesting for any coverage change? [Yes, No]
* * Contract Start Date
* * Description

And on the second step/screen I see message = ‘No previous contract found to perform a change to clone from.’ [existing functionality]
Then I have an option to ‘Start New Contract Request’
And user shall have the following navigation options:

* Finish
** System closes the guided experience
** System auto creates Practice Operations (PO) Contract:
*** Contract Record Type = Practice Operations
*** Status = Draft
*** Contract Owner = <Account Owner>
*** Account Name = <Service Account>
*** Created By = <user who initiated the guided experience>
*** Previous Contract = BLANK
*** Contract Start Date = <Date inputted>
*** Description = <text inputted>
*** -Shift Coverage Change = <checked or unchecked based on question = Are you requesting for any coverage change?>- – REMOVING since this will always be a new contract and should not be limited.
** User is directed to the new Practice Operations (PO) Contract record
* Cancel
** System closes the guided experience
** No Practice Operations (PO) Contract is created
** User is returned to the Service Account record

AC2: Updates rules in 3017 - Ability to clone a ‘Superseded’ PO contract

Given I am a SFO within the Corporate Support Apps
When I am on a Service Account
And the Service Account has Practice Operations Contracts in Status = ‘Superseded’ or ‘Executed’
And I select guided experience = ‘Request Contract Change’
And complete the following required fields & click 'Next' (_existing functionality_)

* Are you requesting for any coverage change?
* Contract Start Date
* Description

Then on the next screen/step the system shall display the following contract information for the contract that will be cloned OR an error message (see [document|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/12tqq953YG1771z_01ITldSqyBTH4xBTm23zSJ_SCNTY/edit?usp=drive_link] for error message scenarios)

* The criteria for selecting the PO Contract to clone is: *Select all the Service Account’s Practice Operations (PO) contracts in Status = ‘Superseded’ or ‘Executed’, order by Contract Start Date (newest to oldest), then select the contract that the inputted ‘Contract Start Date' falls within.*
* Fields to display:
** Contract Number
** Contract Start Date
** Status
** Coverage Change

And when the user selects ‘Create’ then the system shall clone and create new Draft Practice Operations (PO) contract. (_existing functionality_)

+Example for selecting contract to clone:+
Today = 3/2/2026
New Contract’s ‘Contract Start Date’ = *2/15/2026*
Existing PO Contracts Start Date and Status:

* System would evaluate contract #1, 3, & 4 (Status = *‘Superseded’ or ‘Executed’*)
* System would select to *clone off of Contract #1 w/ Status = Superseded*, because new contract start date (2/15/26) falls within Contract #1. User will need to determine if updates are needed in other Executed contracts and make contract changes as needed (Abandon and “Request Contract Change” as needed)

||*Contract*||*Contract Start Date*||*Contract End Date*||*Status*||*Previous Contract*||*Notes:*||
|1|1/1/2023|2/28/2026|*Superseded*|BLANK|Use this contract to clone|
|5 (NEW)|2/15/2026|2/28/2026|Draft|1|NEW|
|2|4/1/2024| |Abandoned|1| |
|3|3/1/2026| |*Executed*|1| |
|4|6/1/2026| |*Executed*|3| |

AC3: Updates story [+PR1050558-3207+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3207] - On the Executed Contract’s ‘Contract Start Date’, reevaluate PO Contracts ‘Contract End Date' & ‘Status’
Given I am a SFO in the Corporate Support apps
And a Practice Operations Contract is approved (Status = ‘Executed')
When the executed contract’s ‘Contract Start Date' is equal to Today or in the past
Then the system shall:

* *Evaluate* all of the Service Account’s Practice Operations (PO) contracts in *Status = ‘Executed’ or ‘Superseded’*
** System shall sort contracts Newest to Oldest (descending)
* Update the *‘Contract End Date’*
** For any ‘Contract Start Date’ that is equal to today or in the past, then set the previous contracts ‘Contract End Date’ = [Prior Contract’s ‘Contract Start Date’ minus 1 day]
* Update the *‘Status’ from ‘Executed’ to ‘Superseded’*
** For any Contract that has a ‘Contract End Date’ that is in the Past, then set ‘Status’ = ‘Superseded’

NOTE AC3: Contracts in Status = ‘Suspended’ should have a 'Contract End Date’ populated. Since we now allow cloning off of superseded contracts as part of AC2, then in AC3 we are updating End Dates to remove gaps between superseded and executed contracts

Example:
Today = {{2026-03-02}}

Contract #5, got executed on 3/2/2026

||*Contract*||*Contract Start Date*||*Contract End Date*
*(Original)*||*Contract End Date*
*(New)*||*Status*
*(Original)*||*Status*
*(New)*||*Note:*||
|4|6/1/2026|BLANK| |Executed| | |
|3|3/1/2026|BLANK| |Executed| | |
|*5 (NEW)*|*2/15/2026*|BLANK|2/28/2026|*Executed (on 3/2/2026)*|Superseded|Contract End Date - updated based on Contract #3, since Start Date (3/1/26) is in the past.
Status - updated based on ‘Contract End Date’ in the past|
|1|1/1/2023|2/28/2026|2/14/2026|Superseded| |Contract End Date - updated based on Contract #5, since Start Date (2/15/26) is in the past.| 		31/Mar/26 11:07 AM   9 Error.png 17/Feb/26 10:57 AM SCP HC Sprint 14 (Phase 2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 13 (Phase 2) 17/Feb/26 2:21 PM 1|i0kny0:v 31/Mar/26 11:07 AM *Tester:* Lisa Kleyla

*Role:* Payroll

*Description:* 

Corrections need to be made to the Practice Operations contract on a service account (which is in executed status) and the changes need same effective date. Since we cannot initiate a new PO with the same effective date, System admin abandoned the executed PO. When we attempt to initiate the corrected PO we receive the attached error. The link to the service account we are working on: [https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001Ps00001Kkam9IAB/view|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001Ps00001Kkam9IAB/view|smart-link]

No permission
 Robert Christensen 0 Robert Christensen 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project     7 EmailLogs1-20260217-090245.png 16/Feb/26 6:03 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.4 (R2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3) 17/Feb/26 4:05 AM 1|i0lr89: 20/Apr/26 9:41 AM *Tester:* Nicole Fontenot

*Role:* Legal – Client Contracting

*Description:* 

The “Send Offboarding Email” step is the final and critical action within the Begin Facility Termination process, serving as the official notification to the company of an upcoming client termination. This step has replaced the former Termination DL process and is now the sole mechanism for communicating termination events.

Currently, when selecting “Send Offboarding Email” from the PNC to release the notification, the email is not being received. This issue was identified during the processing of terminations for Upson Regional Medical Center-EM and Upson Regional Medical Center-HM.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

From the Contract, click on the "Provider Network Contracts" tab

Click into a Provider Network Contract Name

Click on the down arrow next to the buttons in the header and choose "Send Offboarding Email"

Click "Send Email"


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Expected an email that includes the image attached below.

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001ETw8hIAD/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001Ps00001KkEqoIAF%2Fview|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001ETw8hIAD/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001Ps00001KkEqoIAF%2Fview]

PROD Bug: UPHypercare215 - Client Termination Notification Email

 Robert Christensen PR1050558-5809, PR1050558-5810 0 Robert Christensen 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project -AC1: Prevent users from clicking “Submit Contract Request”, to avoid duplicate Approval Work Items from being generated.-

AC1 When Users Click the “Submit Contract Request” on Draft Clinician Contract, if an existing AWI exists, no duplicate is created. 		05/Mar/26 11:22 AM   7 Multiple Tactical AWIs on Contract.PNG 16/Feb/26 11:42 AM SCP HC Sprint 12.4 (R2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 12.6 (R4) 16/Feb/26 12:33 PM 1|i0l97d: 05/Mar/26 11:14 PM *Tester:* Matthew Hebert

*Role:* Clinician Contracting Specialist

*Description:* 

In the tactical AWI queue there were three separate AWIs related to the same Clinician Contract (Elizabeth Galland). We approved one, and the status progressed to the clinician contracting queue. However, the other two AWIs remain in the Tactical queue. I don't want to reject them and inadvertenly cancel the contract record. I spoke to Maci and she thought that there shouldn't have been three AWIs in the first place. What is the appropriate action to take?

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Navigate to Tactical AWI queue, there are mutiple AWIs related to the same Contract record (00470875). In the most recent AWI we selected approve and approved. Contract moved forward in the approval process, but the other two remained.


*Expected vs. Actual:*

There should only be one AWI in the tactical team work items queue related to a contract record at one time. If the recruiter pulls a request back, then the existing AWIs should be canceled. If there will be more than one we need a process related to how to resolve the extra AWIs

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001F4Sr1IAF/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001Ps00001KktJHIAZ%2Fview|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001F4Sr1IAF/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001Ps00001KktJHIAZ%2Fview]

05/Mar/26 9:44 AM Unified Platform Hypercare R4 Done Provider Contracting Done   Critical HyperCareReleaseEligible, SCPHealthCloud, SCPHypercareBug, SCPPRODTicket, TADesignComplete Matthew Hebert   PR1050558-4308, PR1050558-5765, PR1050558-6124, PR1050558-4014 Tyler Anderson Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-5642
No permission
 Robert Christensen PR1050558-6031, PR1050558-6032 AC1: Modify the Lightning Page layout make the fields Read Only on record page when status ≠ Draft

AC2: Update OmniStudio FlexCards to hide delete/trash actions when Recruiter + Status ≠ Draft and Hide Edit for Recruiters when Status ≠ Draft via Flexipage visibility rules as per

AC3: Create: ‘Lock Contract’ permission set to control UI

AC4: Add Lock Contract Permission Set to the following Perm Sets
- Clinician Recruiting 		0 Robert Christensen 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project *Description*: As a Clinician Contract Specialist and/or Clinician Contract Approver, I want the system to lock fields on the clinician contract record once the request is submitted so we can ensure proper approvals are obtained for all clinician contract request details.

AC1: Make ‘Term Sheet Status’ field read only in all statuses for Recruiters.
Given I am a *Recruiter* *with the ‘Lock Contract’ permission* within the Corporate Support apps
When I am on a *Clinician Contract* in *any Status*
Then the following fields on the Details tab are read only on the layout:

* Term Sheet Status (NOTE: on layout in 2 places, Contract Overview and Contract Details sections)
* Contracting Corporate Entity

AC2: Make Clinician Contract Details tab read only for Recruiters when Status is not in Draft
Given I am a *Recruiter with the ‘Lock Contract’ permission* within the Corporate Support apps
When I am on a *Clinician Contract* in *Status ≠ Draft* ( i.e. Submitted/Approved/Contracting - In Progress/Executed/Cancelled)
Then the contract can NOT be edited:

# *Details tab:*
## Section: Contract Overview
### All Fields should be read only on the layout
### Exception to standard fields:
#### Account Name
#### Contract Record Type
#### Contract Owner
## Section: Contract Details
### All Fields should be read only on the layout
## Section: Term & Renewals
### All Fields should be read only on the layout
## Section: Key Dates
### All Fields should be read only on the layout
## Section: System Information
### All Fields should be read only on the layout


AC3: Make Clinician Contract Summary tab read only for all user when Status is not in Draft
Given I am a *internal Corporate Support user with access to the Clinician Contract* within the Corporate Support apps
When I am on a *Clinician Contract* in *Status ≠ Draft* ( i.e. Submitted/Approved/Contracting - In Progress/Executed/Cancelled)
Then on the Summary tab, can NOT be edited the following:

*Summary tab*:

# Remove delete (trash can icons) from:
## Facility
## Type of Rate section

!image-20260227-193237.png|width=595,height=183,alt="image-20260227-193237.png"!

(Business process note on how to unlock [Status = Draft]: Approval will have to be rejected, recalled, or Clinician Contracting Specialist can update from status per 4308)

----

*Refinement Notes:*

{color:#6554c0}*_*Assumption is Admins will have ability to modify and this change only impacts certain Personas outlined below_*{color}{color:#6554c0}***{color}

As a Clinician Contract Specialist and/or Clinician Contract Approver, I want the system to lock fields on the clinician contract record once the request is submitted so we can ensure proper approvals are obtained for all clinician contract request details.

# Modify access to the Recruiter Persona
# Recruiters are not allowed to make modifications to the clinician contract record when the Status is not equal to Draft.
## Inability to modify fields on the Contract details tab or modify configurations on the Summary tab, etc.
##* Don’t Recruiters need to edit the Term Sheet Status field?
## {color:#bf2600} {color}{color:#6554c0}*Not anymore, 5183 took care of that field not auto updating to ‘Not Applicable’ when appropriate. Field updated to ‘Sent' and ‘Signed/Executed’ automatically by DS. -*{color}{color:#6554c0} {color}{color:#bf2600}*[KK] ok, so this assumes that if they are eligible for a Term sheet, then recruiters HAVE to create one, they can’t say “Not Applicable to move it to Clinician Contracting.*{color}
# Once Clinician contract records are no longer in Draft, are fields are locked for editing and no modifications can be made by the Recruiter.
# Retain functionality, if rejected in approvals, record moving back to Draft status and allowing Recruiters to make modifications and resubmit.

{color:#bf2600}*Can we get specific on which persona and even fields/sections that should be read only and when? screenshot of what recruiter sees currently*{color}

* {color:#6554c0}*Persona: Recruiter*{color}
* {color:#6554c0}*Contract Detail image (when in DRAFT): Keep everything the same (fields/sections that are read only or editable) with the exception of the two changes listed below.*{color}
** {color:#6554c0}*Term Sheet Status: read only 100% of the time for this Persona since system is now always updating that value.*{color}{color:#6554c0} {color}
** {color:#6554c0}*Contracting Corporate Entity: read only 100% of the time for this Persona since this is driven by system or other user that has ownership.*{color}{color:#6554c0} {color}
* {color:#6554c0}*Contract Detail image (when NOT equal to DRAFT): Every field / section should be ready only once they have submitted (i.e. Submitted/Approved/Contracting - In Progress/Executed/Cancelled).*{color}{color:#6554c0} {color}
** {color:#6554c0}*Once they submit, if they realize they missed something, the intention is for them to contact who it’s pending with in Approvals or the Contract Specialist team. Approvers can reject it for it to move back to draft for modifications and Contract Specialist can Cancel for them.*{color}{color:#6554c0} {color}

!image-20260224-004235.png|width=667,height=183,alt="image-20260224-004235.png"!

{color:#bf2600}*Shouldn’t we remove edit access from ALL personas once status ≠ Draft for Summary tab*{color}

{color:#6554c0}*Our discussion was mainly around Recruiter persona for the change, but I would agree, no user should be making changes here once status not equal to Draft.*{color}{color:#6554c0} {color}
– will not include making read only for all in this story since need to think about Status field and maybe Key Dates section that may need to be edited.

!image-20260224-003741.png|width=667,height=183,alt="image-20260224-003741.png"! 		05/Mar/26 9:44 AM   9 No tacitcal review .png 13/Feb/26 11:30 AM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 12.6 (R4), SCP HC Sprint 12.3 (R1) 13/Feb/26 1:05 PM 1|i0ktbd: 10/Mar/26 11:35 AM *Tester:* Tara Stogner

*Role:* Clinician Contracting Specialist

*Description:* 

We received a case in the contracting queue that had a Contract Special Term which should have sent the case to the Tactical queue for review but it went straight to the contracting queue instead.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Navigate to Harris Khawaja Contract 00471053. Note special terms on details tab of contract. Note no approval from tactical on approval tab.


*Expected vs. Actual:*

We received a case in the contracting queue that had a Contract Special Term which should have sent the case to the Tactical queue for review but it went straight to the contracting queue instead.

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001F6CTbIAN/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001Ps00001KlWuoIAF%2Fview|https://corehc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800Ps00001F6CTbIAN/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001Ps00001KlWuoIAF%2Fview]

PROD Enhancement: UPHypercare189 - Special Terms in Contract didn't Route to Tactical

None
Production  
30/Mar/26 2:01 PM Unified Platform Sprint 14 Release In Progress Provider Offboarding Unresolved 06/Apr/26 11:38 AM Critical SCPHealthCloud, SCPProdEnhancement, TADesignComplete David Wilkening   PR1050558-5580, PR1050558-5863, PR1050558-5376, PR1050558-6416, PR1050558-1580, PR1050558-1726, PR1050558-2473, PR1050558-6307, PR1050558-6314 Ashlee Johnson-Savoy Ready for Test ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-5641
No permission
 Kristin Koide PR1050558-6152, PR1050558-6153, PR1050558-6166, PR1050558-6177, PR1050558-6178, PR1050558-6179, PR1050558-6432, PR1050558-6433 * 4 new Fields in HPF
** {{Is_Sister_Site_Location__c - Checkbox}} - is true when sister-site links exist for that HPF account
** {{Inactivity_Qualified_270__c - Checkbox}} - is true when crosses over 270 days threshold
** {{Re_Contract__c - Checkbox}}
** {{Aging_Days_Left__c - Number }}
*** calculate inactivity days for current HPF non Sister Location
*** calculate minimum inactivity days across sister sites
*** choose the *minimum of those two* as the merged inactivity duration set {{Aging_Days_Left__c = max(0, 365 - mergedDuration)}}



Logic:

Create a Apex batch job

Sope:

Query all ContactId from HPF where Status NOT IN ('Prospect', 'Candidate', 'Superseded', 'Inactive') AND EffectiveTo == NULL

Execute Scope:

# Query all HPF for the Scope
# Loop through HPF
## Collect Set<Id> → [HPFId]
## Collect Map<PractitionerId, HPF> → [HPF]
## Collect Map<PractitionerId, Set<AccountId> → [PractitionerAccount]
## Collect Map<AccountId> → [ServiceAccountId]
# Utilize the SisterSiteGroupingUtility to get SisterSiteGroup Map<AccountId, String> [accountIdToName];
# Query Contract record for using [HPFId] Query: Select Contract.AccountId, Contract.Employment_Type__c, Min(Contract.Initial_Effective_Date__c) From PNC Where Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c = [HPFId] Group by Contract.AccountId, Contract.Employment_Type__c
## Loop through and collect Map<ClinicianId, Map< Employment_Type__c, Initial_Effective_Date__c>> *[ContractEffectiveDate]*
# Loop Through the [HPF] in Scope *COLLECTING DATES FOR SISTER SITES*
## If [HPF.|http://HPF.id]Id in [accountIdToName]
## Build a Map<ClinicianId, Map<SisterSiteGroupName, LastWorked> *[ClinicianSisterLastWorked]*
### Compare if there is already a LastWorked for the SisterSiteGroupName and if Loop.LastWorked > value in [ClinicianSisterLastWorked] then update [ClinicianSisterLastWorked].LastWorked = Loop.LastWorked
## Build a Map<ClinicianId, Map<SisterSiteGroupName, NextScheduledDate> *[ClinicianSisterNextScheduledDate]*
### Compare if there is already a NextScheduledDate for the SisterSiteGroupName and if Loop.NextScheduledDate > value in [ClinicianSisterNextScheduledDate] then update [ClinicianSisterNextScheduledDate].NextScheduledDate = Loop.NextScheduledDate
## Build a Map<ClinicianId, Map<SisterSiteGroupName, HospitalPrivilegedDate> *[ClinicianSisterHospitalPrivilegedDate]*
### Compare if there is already a HospitalPrivilegedDate for the SisterSiteGroupName and if Loop.HospitalPrivilegedDate > value in [ClinicianSisterHospitalPrivilegedDate] then update [ClinicianSisterHospitalPrivilegedDate].HospitalPrivilegedDate = Loop.HospitalPrivilegedDate
# Loop Through the [HPF] in Scope
## If HPF is Part of Sister Site
### Compare using [ClinicianSisterLastWorked], [ClinicianSisterNextScheduledDate], [ContractEffectiveDate], [ClinicianSisterHospitalPrivilegedDate]
### Based on comparison result stamp Is_Sister_Site_Location__c, Aging_Days_Left__c for each HPF
### If the Date crossed 270 then set
#### Inactivity_Qualified_270__c = Y
#### Re_Contract__c = Y
### If Date Crossed 365 then
#### Status = Inactive
#### Credentialing Status = Inactive
#### Contracting Status = ‘Terminated’
#### Termination Date = <day the automation ran>
#### Inactive Reason = ‘Provider Inactivity >12 Months'
#### Effective To = <set to 365 days from the 'Last Date Worked'>
####* If HPF’s ‘Last Date Worked’ is blank, then set to 365 days from the ‘Initial Effective Date’ (Clinician Contract)
####* If Clinician Contract’s ‘Initial Effective Date’ is blank, then set to 365 days from the HPF’s 'Hospital Privileged Date'

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1vjqFSwhfWa7t5iEd77BGJuMaaoipOF6715djk3N5AE8/edit?usp=sharing|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1vjqFSwhfWa7t5iEd77BGJuMaaoipOF6715djk3N5AE8/edit?usp=sharing] 		0 Kristin Koide 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Create a *report* for *HPFs where “Last Date Worked” is 9 Months (270 days) or more*
Given I am a Internal Corporate Support user with read access to HPFs
When an HPF meeting the following criteria:

* “Status” is *not* “Prospect”, “Candidate”, “Superseded”, or “Inactive”
AND
* “Effective To” = Blank
AND
* *Clinician has not worked for 9 months (270 days); defined as:*
** ‘Last Date Worked’ (HPF) is 9 months (270 days) or more; including all sister sites* that a clinician works at (Account field on HPF where status is not Prospect, Candidate, Superseded or Inactive).
OR
** If ‘Last Date Worked’ (HPF) is 9 months (270 days) or more; including all sister sites*, then check field = ‘Provider Next Scheduled Date’ populated.
*** IF populated, check if the date falls within 365 days from the ‘Last Date Worked’ on this HPF, including all sister sites*.
**** If yes, remove from the -list- report
**** If no, display in report w/ flag or indicate as “Re Contract”
* *A clinician has never worked*:
** If ‘Last Date Worked’ (HPF) is blank and ‘Initial Effective Date’ (Clinician Contract) 9 months (270 days) or more away for this HPF as well as for all sister sites***
OR
** If ‘Last Date Worked’ (HPF) and ‘Initial Effective Date’ (Clinician Contract) are both blank, and the ‘Hospital Privileged Date’ (HPF) is more than 9 months (270 days) away for this HPF as well as for all sister sites***

Then on HPF Report = “3 Month HPF Inactivation”, I can see the following HPF information: (Save report in ‘Public Reports’ folder, along with the ‘Inactive HPF' report)

* HPF Number
* Account (Facility/Service Account)
* Staffing Team (Service Account’s “Staffing Team”)
* Practitioner (Clinician/Provider)
* Professional Title (Clinician’s “Professional Title”)
* Facility Title
* Last Date Worked
* Aging (number of days left before 365 day inactivation occurs)
* Re Contract

AC2: -Updates original story- [+-PR1050558-1580-+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1580] – update Batch to only inactive an HPF (include checking Sister Sites). Updates to the original stories in- {color:#0747a6}-blue-{color}-.- *– SPLIT TO* [+*PR1050558-6307*+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6307]

AC3: *-Send Daily Email-* – updates original- [-PR1050558-1726-|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1726] -inactivation email, we will include both 3 month and 12 mos inactivation in one email.- *– SPLIT TO* [+*PR1050558-6314*+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6314]

AC4: -Once Heather’s job runs to set the Last Date Worked, then trigger the SF Batch ‘DeactivateCliniciansBatch’ job- *– SPLIT TO* [+*PR1050558-6307*+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6307]

----

Note:

* Separate story for sending individual HPF inactivation emails since pertains to all HPFs that get inactivated, not just for ones due to 12 mos of inactivity or never worked. [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5863|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5863|smart-link]
* -Existing functionality per story- [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2473|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2473|smart-link] -facility offboarding case, it’s set to “Last Day”- - removing this note, we are NOT going to create facility offboarding cases, we are either going to send an email (what was done for PFA or a notification)
* This story updates [+PR1050558-1580+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1580] & [+PR1050558-1726+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1726]
* {color:#bf2600}*MS will stop their job*{color} [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5376|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5376|smart-link] once our story is in PROD. –{color:#bf2600}*NEED TO NOTIFY MS team to turn off integration*{color} 		    15 Screenshot 2026-04-07 at 2.36.35 PM.png 13/Feb/26 11:25 AM SCP HC Sprint 14 (Phase 2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 15 (Phase 2) 16/Feb/26 2:35 PM 1|i0knxs:zzi 17/Apr/26 11:07 AM Relates to MS Enhancement = [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5376|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5376|smart-link]. -(Note: Kristi to submit another enhancement request for a new 3 month report)-

As a Data Quality Team member, I want to enhance the Clinician Inactivation process to send a daily email including both HPFs that got inactivated as well as HPFs that are within 3 months of inactivation, so that team members have visibility and can take appropriate action prior to inactivation as needed. I also want to adjust the criteria for auto inactivation, so that we only auto inactive for not working and remove the logic for never worked.

Split story into 3 parts:

# [+PR1050558-5641+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5641] - Covers AC1 - creation of the “3 Month HPF Inactivation” report *(THIS STORY)*
# [+PR1050558-6307+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6307] - Covers AC2 & AC4 - Updating the Inactivation Batch Job & when it’s triggered
# [+PR1050558-6314+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-6314] - Covers AC3 - Daily Email which includes the “Inactive HPF” & “3 Month HPF Inactivation” reports

PROD Enhancement: Update the Clinician Inactivation Process & 3 month report - 1 of 3 (3 month report)

# Create New Queue “VPO Delegates“
# Modify “LeadershipChange_DataServiceIP” in VPO_Check block, append condition and query for members in queue. (If current user in new queue return true)



QA Notes:

in lower environments you can add test users to the new queue to mimic the following Prod users:
Abby - Clinician Experience Specialist

!image-20260223-143916.png|width=329,alt="image-20260223-143916.png"! 		0 Allison Mullins 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Currently: Users who will be requesting leadership changes must add themselves as VPO Account Team members in order to progress the record. These are the admins who do the work on-behalf of the VPOs

Future: Users who will be requesting leadership changes on behalf of their VPO’s will be added to a QUEUE named VPO Delegate. The Omniscript “LeadershipChangeOS” is updated to check that the active user is a VPO OR They are a member of the VPO Delegate Queue.

----

AC1: Update [+PR1050558-2557+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2557]+,+ Allow Account Team Role = VP Operations *and users in new ‘VPO Delegates’ queue to initiate “Leadership Change”* - updates in {color:#0747a6}*blue*{color}
Given I am a VPO (Account Team Role = VP Operations) in the Corporate Support apps
{color:#0747a6}OR a user in the ‘VPO Delegates’ queue{color}
When I navigate to a Service Account record
Then I am able to select ‘Leadership Change’ in the highlights panel
And I am presented with a guided experience [See original stories for more details [+PR1050558-2557+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2557] +&+ [+PR1050558-4337+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4337]]

* {color:#0747a6}Error Message if Not Account Team Role = VP Operations or user in the ‘VPO Delegate’ queue:{color}
** Only the VP Operations {color:#0747a6}or VPO Delegate (queue) {color}can initiate this change.

Activated date not showing historical date on the clinician contracts.
Assign to Data Migration team - will be resolved by James.

 Robert Christensen PR1050558-6249, PR1050558-6182 AC1: Validation Rule in Case

AC2: Record Triggered Flow validation as we need to check other records are completed from Case as well as Task

*AC3* Update the existing record triggered flow that updates the HPF



Create a Activity Record Type : Exit Interview and use for the Task (see Data Dictionary) 		0 Robert Christensen 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project *AC1:* Clinician Offboarding Case - “Last Day” field layout updates for SCP and Facility cases

Given I am an internal Corporate Support User with access Clinician Offboarding Cases (RT: Clinician Offboarding, Type = SCP & Facility)
When I am on a Clinician Offboarding case
Then on field = ‘Last Day’:

* Type = Facility, ‘Last Day' is required
* Type = SCP, ‘Last Day' is conditionally required to close* the case (* Close means: Status = Closed, Closed Reason = Complete)

*AC2*: Update SCP Clinician Offboarding Task to populate the “Offboarding Decision” field (Updates original story [+PR1050558-1733+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1733]) w/ new rule to close this task

Given I am a Account Team Member = Regional Director of NP/PA Services, RN/LPN Manager, or Regional Medical Officer EM or HM within in the Corporate Support apps
When the “Initiate Termination Request” Guided Experience is completed from the Clinician record (person account)
And a new SCP Clinician Offboarding case is created
Then the system shall either:

* {color:#0747a6}If a ‘Exit Interview’ task exists, then prevent the user from updating the ‘Exit Interview’ task Status = ‘Completed' or ‘Canceled’ until they complete the NEW required ‘Offboarding Decision’ field on the ‘Exit Interview’ task{color}
** {color:#0747a6}New picklist field = ‘Offboarding Decision’ in new Section = ‘SCP Offboarding’{color}
*** {color:#0747a6}Values: No Longer Exiting, Confirmed Exit{color}
*** {color:#0747a6}ONLY display this section & field on ‘Exit Interview’ tasks{color}
*** {color:#0747a6}Required to be populated to close the task (Status = ‘Completed' or ‘Canceled’){color}
*** {color:#0747a6}Error Message: An Offboarding Decision is required to close the task.{color}
** {color:#0747a6}Upon the user populating the ‘Offboarding Decision’ and closing the ‘Exit Interview’ task, update the SCP Clinician Offboarding Case’s ‘Offboarding Decision’ field with the value captured on the task.{color}

*OR*

* If there is no ‘Exit Interview’ task, then auto populate SCP Case field 'Offboarding Decision’ = “*Confirmed Exit*”

NOTE for AC2: see original story 1733 for other existing SCP tasks and criteria for task creation. Link to SharePoint file, see ‘Clinician Offboarding’ Tab: [Clinician Onboarding_Offboarding - Cases and Tasks|https://schumachergroup.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/teams/UnifiedPlatform-ReGenesis/Shared%20Documents/Program%20Documentation/Program%20Execution/1.%20Build,%20QA,%20Refinement/Client%20%26%20Clinician%20Experience/Clinician%20Onboarding_Offboarding%20-%20Cases%20and%20Tasks.xlsx?d=w22ea8633b18a4e0cb5220132e36559eb&csf=1&web=1&e=0qksp1]

*AC3:* Update existing [+PR1050558-2473+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2473] - Remove HPF update logic associated with the SCP Offboarding case Offboarding Decision = *Confirmed Exit.* Updating HPF records should ONLY be triggered as part of the Facility Offboarding updates to avoid conflicts and ensure each HPF is updated individually based on specific Facility Termination information captured as part of the “Initiate Termination Request” Guided Experience.

Given I am a Corporate Support user in the Corporate Support apps
When I navigate to a Clinician Offboarding case where Type = SCP
And -Then I am able to update the- Offboarding Decision field is set to *Confirmed Exit*
{color:#0747a6}And{color} {color:#0747a6}Case Status = Closed, Closed Reason = Complete{color}
Then -And- the following updates happen automatically:

* -HPF updates- - {color:#0747a6}Remove this logic{color}
** -Effective To = Last Day-
** -Contracting Status = Termination In Progress-
** -Anticipated Termination Date = Last Day-
* -Offboarding Case updates (occur when all child tasks are closed) – {color:#0747a6}Remove this logic, covered under {color}[+PR1050558-5231+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5231] & [+PR1050558-5232+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5232] to {color:#0747a6}ensure all facility cases are closed before allowing SCP case to close to get the appropriate ‘Last Day’. {color}
** -Status = Closed-
** -Closed Reason = Complete-
* -HPF updates (occur when Effective To is in the past)- - {color:#0747a6}Remove this logic{color}
** -Status = Inactive-
** -Contracting Status = Terminated-
** -Termination Date = day automation ran-
* Person Account updates -(occur when Effective To on all HPFs is in the past)- – ({color:#0747a6}Occurs when the SCP Case’s ‘Last Day’ is in the past) - NOTE: we have a process which populate with the ‘Last Day’ based on the latest/furthest Facility Offboarding Cases ‘Last Day’ per {color}[{color:#0747a6}+PR1050558-4706+{color}|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4706]{color:#0747a6}.{color}
** Status = Inactive

{color:#ff991f}*AC4*{color}{color:#ff991f}: Add History Tracking on Cases (Split to: {color}[{color:#ff991f}+PR1050558-5828+{color}|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5828]{color:#ff991f}){color}

----

REFINEMENT NOTES ONLY, NOT PART OF THE AC:

SCP Offboarding Case

* -“Offboarding Decision” is conditionally required to close- (Not needed, we have validation on task) - {color:#bf2600}confirm with business if we need extra validation or just case validation will work? Split to {color}[https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5829|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5829|smart-link], to unblock this story 		27/Mar/26 1:20 PM   8 Screenshot 2026-03-25 at 8.24.03 AM.png 11/Feb/26 1:59 PM SCP HC Sprint 14 (Phase 2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP HC Sprint 13 (Phase 2), SCP HC Sprint 12.3 (R1) 11/Feb/26 2:31 PM 1|i0knxt: 27/Mar/26 1:20 PM *Tester:* Kristin Koide

*Role:* IT

*Description:* 

Per story PR1050558-2473, the system should update the HPF to Status = 'Inactive' on the 'Effective To' date.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Persona: MSL, Clinician Experience Specialist, or Data Quality team initiates the 'Initiate Termination Request' from a clinician record.
2. in the 'Initiate Termination Request' guided experience select full SCP termination and ensure that 'Last Day' is in the future. (ex. 3/1/2026)
2. First ensure that the Facility Offboarding tasks are completed and case is closed
3. then ensure that the SCP Offboarding tasks are complete and case is closed with Offboarding Decision: Confirmed Exit


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Expected Result: system should update the HPF record fields when Effective To is in the past)

Status = Inactive
Contracting Status = Terminated
Termination Date = day automation ran

Actual Results: HPF record updates are happening immediately instead of happening on the 'Effective To' Date:
HPF updates (occur when Effective To is in the past)

Status = Inactive
Contracting Status = Terminated
Termination Date = day automation ran

*Record Link:* 

Not Provided

PROD Enhancement: UPHypercare133 - HPF 'Status' is getting set to 'Inactive' before the 'Effective To' date

None

*Role:* Recruiter

*Description:* 

We need a solution for a recruiter to submit a request/Clinician Contract that will have the same Start Date as an executed Clinician Contract. This is common for a recruiter to need to either re-request something that has already been signed correcting something that was not right (change a rate), or adding something additional to an executed contract (leadership stipend or bonus) all with the same effective date.

PROD Enhancement: UPHypercare73 - Need a solution for recruiters to request a new contract with the same start date as an executed contract

Create a new formula field on the applicant record - please update DD with new formula field created 		0 Kristin Koide 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: As a Recruiter, when I create an Intake Case via the automated process (ATS Applicant record Stage = Awaiting Intake Form) I want the Clinician Profile Update Assessment to be based off of the Employment Type of the Job’s Facility Title, so that the CTI section can be displayed when Employment Type = Independent Contractor. This updates original story [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-231|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-231|smart-link].

Given I am a Clinician with an open Intake Form case created when ATS Stage field on an ATS Applicant record to *Awaiting Intake Form*
When I log in to the Core Connect portal
Then I am able to complete the *Clinician Profile Update Assessment* Intake Form
And the CTI (Corporation and Tax Information) section shall be displayed when Employment Type = Independent Contractor (IC), based on the *Job.Facility_Title_Lookup__c* not *HealthcarePractitionerFacility.Facility_Title__c* to determine *Employment Type*
[See story 231 for CTI section information]


NOTE: HPF record does not get auto created until much further in the recruiting process (Stage= Offer)

 marla.pilpel PR1050558-6181 0 marla.pilpel 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Updates for Employment Type = “*Employee*”, Time Type = ‘*Part Time*' , & Clinician’s Professional Title = *NP or PA*

And the Clinician has a Professional Title = NP or PA
And does NOT have any contracts in Status = Draft or Executed
And I click “Contract Request” guided experience
And on the ‘Employee Type Selection’ step, I select Employment Type = “*Employee*” & Time Type = ‘*Part Time*'
And on the ‘Contract Selection’ step, I select “*Start New Request*”

 Robert Christensen When creating the Child case Use the Case.ParentId field to populate the Parent Case

Record Triggered Flow Use in Case after update ((Type = SCP, RT = _Clinician_Offboarding_, Case.Status = Closed) use Case.ParentId to query the child records status Status != Closed (count) if the Count> 0 and also check the child Task for Case (Type = SCP, RT = _Clinician_Offboarding_) then prevent the Parent Case (Type = SCP, RT = _Clinician_Offboarding_) closure 		0 Robert Christensen 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a MSL or data quality team member in the Corporate Support apps
When the Clinician offboarding process is initiated as part of the guided experience [PR1050558-306: Clinician Offboarding - Termination Details|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-306]
And Clinician Offboarding case(s) are created (Type = SCP, Facility)
Then the system shall prevent the user from updating the case ‘Status’ = ‘Closed’ until all of the child tasks are closed (task Status = ‘Completed’ or 'Canceled')
And display error message: Please ensure all child tasks are completed. This will close the Offboarding case.



See tab = ‘[Clinician Offboarding|https://schumachergroup.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/teams/UnifiedPlatform-ReGenesis/Shared%20Documents/Program%20Documentation/Program%20Execution/1.%20Build,%20QA,%20Refinement/Client%20%26%20Clinician%20Experience/Clinician%20Onboarding_Offboarding%20-%20Cases%20and%20Tasks.xlsx?d=w22ea8633b18a4e0cb5220132e36559eb&csf=1&web=1&e=1X5hpM]’ for list of tasks under each Type of Case 		26/Feb/26 10:22 PM   6 image-20260227-032233.png 26/Jan/26 3:33 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3) 28/Jan/26 5:31 PM 1|i0jlef: 26/Feb/26 10:22 PM *Tester:* Kristi Bouillion, Matthew Hebert

*Role:* Data Quality

*Test Case ID:* Gold Set- final tab- 25- terminate EE all site (involuntary)

*Description:* 

Validation Rule Needed: Any Parent Case should not be able to be completed until all associated child cases and/or activities have been completed. The users should work and complete all child cases timely and allow the system to auto-close (complete) the parent case.

Robert Dobson in UAT2 was our example

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001bf00000S3wNRAAZ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FHealthcarePractitionerFacility%2F0bSbf000000ALHFEA4%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001bf00000S3wNRAAZ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FHealthcarePractitionerFacility%2F0bSbf000000ALHFEA4%2Fview|smart-link]

UAT Enhancement: C&CUAT402 Validation Rule Needed: Any Parent Case should not be able to be completed, until all activities have been completed

None
 
26/Feb/26 10:23 PM Unified Platform Hypercare R4 Done Provider Offboarding Done   Critical SCPHealthCloud, SCPUATEnhancement, TADesignComplete Kristi Bouillion   PR1050558-5232, PR1050558-5580, PR1050558-5903, PR1050558-2421, PR1050558-2462, PR1050558-2473 Ashlee Johnson-Savoy Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-5231
No permission
 Robert Christensen When creating the Child (Type = Facility, RT = Clinician_Offboarding) case Use the SCP case Case.Id field to populate the Parent Case on the Facilty case Case.ParentId

Existing Logic: All Child Task(s) for Case (Type = SCP, RT = Clinician_Offboarding) is Closed via Record Triggered Flow in Task the Case gets Closed

Now Logic: All Child Task(s) for Case (Type = SCP, RT = Clinician_Offboarding) is Closed via Record Triggered Flow in Task the Case gets Closed only when the All the Child Task(s) are closed and also all the Child Cases is also closed

Also when the Child Case is Closed check if all the Child Case is closed and also all child task for the parent Case is closed then Close the Parent Case 		0 Robert Christensen 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a MSL or data quality team member in the Corporate Support apps
When the Clinician offboarding process is initiated as part of the guided experience [PR1050558-306: Clinician Offboarding - Termination Details|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-306]
And both SCP Offboarding & Facility Offboarding case(s) are created
Then the system shall prevent the user from updating the SCP Offboarding (Type = SCP) case Status = ‘Closed’ until all of the Clinician’s Facility Offboarding (Type = Facility) case(s) are in Status = ‘Closed’.
And display error message: Please close all Facility Offboarding case(s) by ensuring child tasks are completed OR updating the ‘Offboarding Decision’ prior to closing the SCP Offboarding case.
And upon creation of the Facility Offboarding case(s), the system shall auto populate the “Parent Case” field with the SCP Case. 		26/Feb/26 10:23 PM   6 image-20260227-032059.png 26/Jan/26 3:16 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3) 28/Jan/26 5:31 PM 1|i0jldz: 27/Feb/26 8:48 AM *Tester:* Kristi Bouillion, Matthew Hebert

*Role:* Data Quality

*Test Case ID:* Gold Set- final tab- 25- terminate EE all site (involuntary)

*Description:* 

We do not want the SCP Offboarding Case to be eligible to be closed until all Facility Offboarding Cases are completed/closed for a provider, as this will cause the HPFs to not be inactivated. The validation rule should stop the user from marking the parent SCP Offboarding Case with a status = closed, when any associated facility offboarding boarding case(s) is not equal to closed; otherwise, the HPF will remain in its current state and not terminated/inactivated.

Robert Dobson in UAT2 was our example.

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001bf00000S3wNRAAZ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FHealthcarePractitionerFacility%2F0bSbf000000ALHFEA4%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001bf00000S3wNRAAZ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FHealthcarePractitionerFacility%2F0bSbf000000ALHFEA4%2Fview|smart-link]

UAT Enhancement: C&CUAT401 Validation Rule Needed: SCP Offboarding Case Should not be able to complete until all Facility Offboarding Cases are completed (Status =Closed)

None
 
09/Feb/26 3:23 PM   In Progress Provider Recruiting Unresolved   High HyperCareReleaseEligible, SCPHealthCloud, SCPUATEnhancement Matthew Hebert   PR1050558-1696 Zach Roberts On Hold ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-5222
No permission
 Robert Christensen 0 Robert Christensen Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project As a recruiter that amends an existing active IC NPPA agreement, I want the system to NOT extend the End Date by one year if an End Date exists, so that the terms of the original agreement are still compliant.     4 23/Jan/26 12:45 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3) 09/Feb/26 9:51 AM 1|i0jjsn: 05/Mar/26 1:21 PM *Tester:* Matthew Hebert

*Role:* Recruiter

*Test Case ID:* IC NPPA terminates and new Contract start date creates a gap in coverage

*Description:* 

For an IC NPPA, the original Clinician Contract record/HPF will have an End Date/Effective To Date one year from the original effective date. As built, if the Recruiter submits a request for the new future contract and uses an effective date of one month after the original end date, then the system will push the end date of the original contract to the day before the effective date of the new contract. While that is appropriate for contracts that do not already have an End Date (evergreen contracts) it cannot do this for a fixed term contract, since the result would be an inconsistency between the paper contract and the system data

UAT Enhancement: C&CUAT394 GAP Scenario

None

 Jasmine Moradi PR1050558-5211, PR1050558-5212 Added examples for better understanding [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/19DsMwfjs0JSxk6DUu0KoafnznoQSOS83XHbvXCKMEA4/edit?gid=1493984067#gid=1493984067|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/19DsMwfjs0JSxk6DUu0KoafnznoQSOS83XHbvXCKMEA4/edit?gid=1493984067#gid=1493984067|smart-link]



AC1: {color:#bf2600}*Important: Write this code after all the initial processing is DONE (as is built today)!!! (we do no what to disturb the current code or logic)*{color}

{color:#bf2600}*Batch Job 1 (Update - Creating Client_Change_Management__c records existing job):*{color}

Create a separate Queueable job for this feature

*Get PO Contract Data*

# Using the ‘Current Contract from Batch’ Query PCA record Type= Shift and Cloned From = NULL
## Get all the new shifts that got added PCA.Existing Rate Increased? = False AND PCA.Existing Rate Decreased? = False AND PCA.Existing Shift Type Added? = True
## Get the PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Shift_Name__c (all unique Shift_Name__c)
### e.g. Day 1 = 100, Day 2 = 100 Day 3 = 100, Night 1 = 200, Night 2 = 200, Night 3 = 200 could have been added into the Contract and the Rates

*Get Clinician Contract Data*

# Query Client_Change_Management__c records that are created for the current Contract that we are working and get the [HPF.Id|http://HPF.Id], HPF.PractitionerId
## Select Id,Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.PractitionerId, Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c from Client_Change_Management__c where Process Type = Automation AND Contract__c = ‘Current Contract from Batch’
# Using the HPF.PractitionerId query the Active Contract for that Clinician
# Query all the Shifts from [Clinician]PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Shift_Name__c in shift added in new shift (Step 1.b)
## If no shift of any of the Type (from 1.a) existing in exists then set Client_Change_Management__c.Status__c = Canceled
### e.g. no Day or Night is available for the Clinician Contract as Shift Type *(Existing Clinician Data)*
## If all the rates for each Type Shift_Name__c (Day, Night) is same in Client Contract then +*Do Nothing (Same Rate as Facility for Each Type e.g. Type = Day) - Skip Record*+
### e.g. Day 1 = 100, Day 2 = 100, Night 1 = 200, Night 2 = 200 *(Existing Clinician Data)*
### Expected Day 1 = 100, Day 2 = 100, Night 1 = 200 add Day 3 = 100, Night 3 = 200
## If all the rates for each Type Shift_Name__c (Day, Night) is same in Client Contract then +*Do Nothing (Higher Rate then Facility for Each Type e.g. Type = Day)- Skip Record*+
### e.g. Day 1 = 120, Day 2 = 120, Night 1 = 200, Night 2 = 200 *(Existing Clinician Data)*
### Expected: Day 1 = 120, Day 2 = 120, Night 1 = 200, Night 2 = 200 add *Day 3 = 120, Night 3 = 200*
## If +*any*+ of rates for each Type Shift_Name__c (Day, Night) is not same in -Client- Clinician Contract Set = Client_Change_Management__c.Process_Type__c = Manual and Create new Task (Check +*AC2*+)
### e.g. Day 1 = 100, Day 2 = 110, Night 1 = 200, Night 2 = 200 (if at one if the Type e.g. Day is having mixed Rate then just make it Manual) *(Existing Clinician Data)*
### Expected Covert Client_Change_Management__c as Manual
## If all the rates for each Type Shift_Name__c (Day, Night) is same in Client Contract then +*Do Nothing (Higher Rate then Facility for Each Type e.g. Type = Day)- Skip Record*+
### e.g. Day 1 = +*90*+, Day 2 = +*90*+ *(Existing Clinician Data*)
### Expected: Day 1 = 90, Day 2 = 90, *add Day 3 = 90 do not add a Night 3 = 200*
## -If all the rates for each Type Shift_Name__c (Day, Night) Is not available in Clinicians Contracts-
### -Client_Change_Management__c.Process_Type__c = Canceled-



{color:#bf2600}*Batch Job 2 (Update - Create PCA Records for Clinician Contract):*{color}

Change the Batch Job that picks the Client_Change_Management__c = Automation and update the Clinician Contract

# Existing Logic: If Clinician Rate> Facility Rate then Clinician Rate else Facility Rate - Remove
# New Logic: assign Clinician Rate only - Add

{color:#bf2600}*Clinician Contract Omni Script Update:*{color}

AC3: Create the flag in Contract Request Process Coverage_Change_Flag__c as specific in AC 		0 Jasmine Moradi 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project +AC1: Check Practice Operations Contract Change+

Given I am a SFO in the Corporate Support Apps
When a Client Contract (RT: Practice Operations) Status gets updated to ‘Executed’
Then the type of change (manual, agent, or automation) is identified based on the following logic ([+link+|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/19DsMwfjs0JSxk6DUu0KoafnznoQSOS83XHbvXCKMEA4/edit?usp=sharing] to supporting document)

+AC2: Check Clinician Contract Change+

Given I am a SFO in the Corporate Support Apps
When a Client Contract (RT: Practice Operations) Status gets updated to ‘Executed’
Then the system shall generate one task for each of the affected HPFs that follow the Manual path:

* Task record type: Default
* Assigned To: Account Team - Clinician Experience Specialist
* Subject: New Clinician Contract Needed - Coverage Change
* Name: [Clinician]
* Related To: [Client Change Management] _note: this has HPFs linked_
* Comments: Review clinician rate for coverage only change. This clinician has rates that are different from other rates with the same type. If an adjustment is needed, request a new clinician contract record.

+AC3: Skip Agreement Generation (Contract Record Creation Only)+

Given I am a Recruiter in the Corporate Support Apps
When I generate a clinician contract
Then I am able to use the Coverage Change Flag below the SCP Book Travel field (default Unchecked)

_Note: Contracting Specialists can manually update Contract records to Executed Status when they see that this flag is checked_ 		26/Feb/26 1:16 PM   9 Outlook-A blue cir.png 21/Jan/26 1:42 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.4 (R2), SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP HC Sprint 12.3 (R1) 11/Feb/26 8:03 AM 1|i0jfvz: 26/Feb/26 3:13 PM As a SFO, I want the system to automatically update related Clinician executed Contracts based on specific type of updates made to the *Practice Operations* contract record, so that Clinician Contracts stay in sync with the Client contract and contract generation in QGenda can be appropriately triggered 23/Jan/26

 Robert Christensen 0 Robert Christensen 0 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a SFO in the Corporate Support apps
When I clone a Practice operation contract, and I specify a employment type, the cloned rate lines from the previous contract should be filtered to only show rate lines that match the employment type so that I don’t accidentally choose to include one of the invalid rate lines cloned from the previous contract. 		    6 IC Shift displayed for EE.png 21/Jan/26 12:25 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT) 21/Jan/26 4:13 PM 1|i0jfqf: 11/Mar/26 1:31 PM *Tester:* Maci Broussard

*Role:* N/A

*Test Case ID:* MAIN-011

*Description:* 

Completing a rate increase through Request Contract Change on Service Account for EE NPPAs. During specify rates, system displaying shift for IC NPPA. In reviewing the PO, DC1 only applies to IC NPPAs.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Log in as Abby Stutes
2. Navigate to WK Pierremont Health Center - EM
3. Request Contract Change effective 4/1/26
4. Selected "no" for coverage change
5. New PO created, navigate to Summary Tab and Specify Rates
6. Selected: Type of Rate = Shift, Clinician Type = NPPA, Employment Type = Employee
7. Next screen (displaying impacted shifts) included DC1 which was for IC NPPAs (not EEs) (IMAGE)


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Would expect system to only display shifts meeting criteria selected (Shift / NPPA / Employee)

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000Nu089AAB/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UC1IPAA1%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000Nu089AAB/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UC1IPAA1%2Fview]

 Robert Christensen 0 David Wilkening 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 09/Apr/26 12:19 PM   8 image-20260320-180510.png 21/Jan/26 12:21 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.5 (R3), SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP HC Sprint 17 (Phase 2) 21/Jan/26 4:15 PM Data Issue 1|i0jfq7: 09/Apr/26 12:19 PM *Tester:* Sheila Robson

*Role:* Payroll

*Test Case ID:* IC Termination

*Description:* 

CTI has to have an expiration date for the termination to fire to Workday. In our test case the CTI that was migrated without a termination date so the provider was not sent to Workday.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Winston Cromwell (806847) Requested term for all HPFs and checked SCP Termination box. Put in termination date of 1/20. The integration is set to trigger in one day. The supplier request did not make it to Workday.


*Expected vs. Actual:*

A supplier request should have been sent to Workday. It will be important to have expiration dates on the CTIs when they are migrated.

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001bf00000S4ImnAAF/view|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001bf00000S4ImnAAF/view]



SME/Reporters: Kristi, Shelia, Amber, Lisa. All meet with Maci about this last week I believe and gave [~accountid:712020:8df36401-937d-4482-b0d6-ea916552ba0c] additional requirements. (additional requirements should be added to story 5482, not this bug)
